channel.title: Comments for page "A Convincing Lyre"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Convincing Lyre"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry to say that you're giving me too much credit for this! SCP-2337 is only referenced as one of the skips that breaches containment at the end of the tale - I didn't have any other plans for Spanko, I'm afraid.

In fact, Hennessey isn't even explicitly made deaf by hearing SCP-012 played - he just became deaf some time after hearing it, although the inference that it caused his deafness is possible to draw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was questioning WHY the dr. spanko tag was added to this

then i realized
the only thing scip i know that probably can perform the song is SCP-2337, evidenced by the word "cacaphonic", and how only one person survived it, and now they're deaf

very clever
+1
Hey, sometime last month this hit 100 in Tale-years! 50 upvotes, no downvotes - thanks everyone :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Kalinin - I'm pleased you liked it.

This sets up a clear story, introduces to us to the main players in a manner that isn't just a straight info-dump…

One of the interesting things about using the existing On Mount Golgotha canon was the fact that it made the introductory tale easier - Emma is a perfect audience surrogate, so having her meet Eta-11 allows a relatively natural entry for the characters (as opposed to introducing them through narration).

I like how carefully constructed this was in relation to other works; the cross-references all make sense, and represent a diversity of material from the wiki

There are a few sets of cross references (the "best tunes" throwaway lines, the containment breach at the end) and they all came from simply searching through the "acoustic", "musical" and "auditory" tags for anything that fit the bill. I really enjoyed that - it was a great way to find writing that I'd never come across otherwise, and I hope other teams found the same thing in their planning process. The containment breach, in particular, probably represents the entire set of skips that could plausibly escape under their own steam and require Eta-11's help to recapture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Solid piece of writing. This sets up a clear story, introduces to us to the main players in a manner that isn't just a straight info-dump, and gives us a little characterization to chew on as readers. I'm not an overarching canon person, but I like how carefully constructed this was in relation to other works; the cross-references all make sense, and represent a diversity of material from the wiki.

Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Emma and Roger primarily, but by including Emma, you are including everything in the On Mount Golgotha Canon as well. This is the only contest series that I have read so far (I am still not quite done reading every one) that feels connected like this one does. Your team is the only one that used the opportunity to really build on existing narratives, and the only one that really built your contest entries around planned future work as well.

As far as SCP-2337 goes, he is logically in Eta-11s wheelhouse, since his sonic effects are really the most immediately hazardous thing about him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Thanks, sirpudding.

weaves the MTF into existing narrative threads and established characters

I presume that you meant Emma, although with only two Tales prior to this, I'm not sure how established she is. Of course, you might have meant Dr Spanko - I have been quite surprised that no-one has called me out for throwing a reference to him into this tale!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a supremely entertaining yarn, and does two things better than any other single contest entry, I think:

  • Sets up the MTF with a clear idea of their theme, their size, their service culture, their place in the Foundation as a whole and the specific characters that comprise it.
  • Seemlessy weaves the MTF into existing narrative threads and established characters.

Of all the MTFs in the contest, I think Eta-11 is the one that I would be most likely to just have a use for at some point (rather than needing either some rather odd circumstances or convoluted narrative reasoning) and if a goal of the contest was to create new MTFs that would be modular elements for future authors, this is the most successful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Decibelle - the link to An Impenitent Thief has now been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like everything about this, and look forward to the continuation. Nicely done, +1

item.title: @Psul
item.content:encoded:
Then you may be interested

I am VERY interested. All is well in the world, then! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to say that it's fine; I already gave a ruling to Basic Team on it, so you will be fine as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
cut off abruptly when things get interesting again.

Looking forward to more!

Then you may be interested to know that a direct sequel tale to this, dealing with the containment breach of SCP-339, has already been drafted :)

Actually, Decibelle - could I please get a ruling on whether editing this tale to include a link to the sequel (once posted) is within the rules?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful.

Harks back to the glory days of the old MTFs, with just enough new thrown in to keep things interesting. Looking forward to more!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellently written. Well, of course, it's Psul! ;> Especially the beginning, with the description of the 'concert'. The rest is also very good. I'm a bit miffed at the end… sounds cut off abruptly when things get interesting again. Not that they're uninteresting just before… Oh never mind! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been looking forward to more on Mount Golgotha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gets me into the story built around 012 much better than Bigger Than Jesus ever did, although I didn't really feel all that engaged in it throughout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was an amazing read. This is really making me look forward to the upcoming MTF contest contributions. +1

item.title: Enter the Savage Beasts!
item.content:encoded:
MTF Eta-11 take to the stage in the 2016 MTF Contest! As well as being the lead performers in this piece, they will also be part of the ensemble for "On Mount Golgotha", the ongoing canon relating to SCP-012.

You don't need to have done any of the pre-reading about Junior Researcher Emma Stark's investigation of SCP-012, but in case you're interested, Emma was introduced in The Journal of K M Sandoval, and this tale follows immediately after Bigger Than Jesus.

Much thanks to the Site-19 String Quartet – WrongJohnSilver, CumaeanSibyl and Smapti – for their feedback and encouragement. Thanks also to MrWrong for review and comments on the forums.

This is the first of the MTF Eta-11 contest entries. If you are interested in reading on, below is a recommended reading order:

  • A Convincing Lyre
  • An Impenitent Thief
  • Project Proposal 2014-2112: "Dreams of Failure"
  • SCP-2402
  • Enjoy the Silence
  • The Musical Suspects
